name: Pyreheart (Infernal Domain RPG)
description:
🗺️ Realm Name:
Pyreheart
Also called The Dominion of Ember Oaths, The Hollow Pyre, or among sinners, The Low Court.

🌍 Realm Overview:
Pyreheart is a polity forged inside a living inferno where law is barter with pain and politics are adjudicated by flame. It’s a place of contract bargains sealed in ash, where ambition is measured by how many vows you can bind to fire without being consumed.

🧭 Geography & Environment:

  • Terrain (mountains, oceans, forests, etc.):
    Black basalt plateaus, rivers of slow-burning tar and brimstone, glass plains formed from cooled lava, obsidian pinnacles, and sink-basins called pyres that belch ash and scream-gases.

  • Climate & Weather:
    Constant heat haze and ember-swirls, sulfurous storms, raining sparks, and seasonal “cold nights” where the fire retreats and the domain’s true voices grow louder.

  • Natural Resources:
    Emberstone (a gem-like slag that stores heat and oath-signatures), brim-iron (metal tempered by hellfire), black glass (used for mirrors and sigils), and will-ash (ashes that preserve sworn intent).

  • Major Landmarks or Regions:

    • The Court Kiln — a stepped amphitheater of furnaces where verdicts are burned into law.

    • Ashmarket — open bazaar over a lava seam where goods are weighed against suffering.

    • The Sootwastes — plains where exiled promises crystallize into bitter formations.

    • The Rift of Echoed Bargains — a chasm lined with hung oaths that still whisper.

  • Map (optional):
    Available if you want a printable handout.

👑 Factions & Power Structures:

  • Main Kingdoms/Empires/Tribes:

    • The Pyre-Consulate (council of flame-brokers)

    • House Cinderhail (dominant blood-oligarchy)

    • The Tally of Ashes (clerks and enforcers who read and tally oaths)

    • The Emberbound Guilds (craftsmen, forgers, and contract-smiths)

  • Government Styles:
    Oligarchic-legalism: authority flows from publicly registered oaths. Offices are tenure-based and can be burned away — literally — if their holder fails. Law is ceremony; judges preside with bellows and brand.

  • Military Presence:
    Legionaries in scale-smelt armor, fire-lancers, brimstone artillery, and oathbound legions — undead or construct troops animated by enforced vows.

  • Religious or Cult Influence:
    Cults worship the First Contract — a primordial pact etched in ash. Clergy function as notaries, priests as inquisitors; some sects practice sacrificial theology, others aim to transmute suffering into salvation.

  • Major Conflicts or Wars:

    • The Char Accord Revolt (an uprising by indebted clans)

    • The Scouring of Vows (systemic purge of secret oaths threatening the Consulate)

    • Border skirmishes with cool realms seeking to quench Pyreheart’s influence

🧙 Magic & Technology:

  • Magic System (if any):
    Pirotheurgy — signature magic binding spoken intent to flame. Incantations, sigil-burns, and thermal runes create enforceable effects activated by heat and moral weight.

  • Source of Magic or Power:

    • The First Ash (a fragment of the founding conflagration)

    • Will-ash charged by sincere vows

    • Emberstone lattices that store and release contract-energy

  • Technological Level (medieval, steampunk, post-apocalyptic, etc.):
creator: abbey
